content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory can be redesigned to mimic the prefrontal-hippocampal interaction that underlies human memory retrieval, integrating Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to split memory function into a hippocampus-like long-term knowledge vector store and a prefrontal cortex-like active learning ranking module that dynamically selects which unlabeled memory entries to re-encode for better downstream generation performance. This creates a curiosity-driven active RAG system that proactively expands its own relevant memory instead of only retrieving from a static pre-built corpus.
